observations.recommendation:
1.12 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE, faithfully:
a. Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical
count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit
team not later than January 31, of each year.
b. Require the General Services Officer to maintain and update his property
cards per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Section
119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update his property
ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to
Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant
reconcile their property cards and property ledger cards, respectively.

2.15 In view of the foregoing, we strongly recommended that LCE instruct
the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Faithfully observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting
for inventories as provided under the MNGAS.
b. Secure to have a stockroom provided for the inventory items of the
agency to protect them from spillage/spoilage and unauthorized
use.
c. Stop the practice of recording/charging regular purchases of
supplies and materials immediately to the expense accounts and
should maintain on a current basis in the General Ledger (GL)
and Inventory Account for all purchases of supplies and materials
for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within
the accounting period. Issuance thereof shall be recorded based on
the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued.

3.8 We recommended the Management to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal
Assessor and Municipal Treasurer in establishing the accurate
amount of basic RPT Receivable from each taxpayer;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect appropriate
adjustment on the affected accounts by drawing a Journal Entry
Voucher to adjust the amount of recorded to reconcile the asset
and liability accounts; and
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal
Accountant with a duly certified list showing the names of the
taxpayers and the actual due and collective for the year which
shall serve as the basis for setting up the RPT receivable due for
the year.

4.8 We recommended that the payment of procured items should be made
directly to the supplier/dealer and stop the practice of paying procured
supplies and materials thru reimbursement and the appropriate
procurement procedure be faithfully adhered to. Furthermore, all the
required documents should be complied and complete to ensure that the
approval of these disbursement vouchers should first be sought before
payments are made.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reclassify the PPE accounts below ₱50,000.00 threshold to its
appropriate semi-expendable properties and expenses; and
b. Adjust the opening balance in the Statement of Changes in Net
Assets/Equity in compliance to the COA Circular No. 2022-004,
dated May 31, 2022.

6.5 We recommended that the General Services Officer (GSO) facilitate the
issuance of the accompanying ICS of the semi-expendable properties,
pursuant to Section 4.1 of the COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated May 31,
2022.

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to
implement immediately the proper numbering of DVs and JEVs for a
good internal control of financial transactions.

8.7 We recommended that the LCE who is primarily responsible for all
government funds and property to instruct the Municipal Engineer
and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to
submit the necessary documents on various projects undertaken by
the LGU.

The Municipal Accountant should also submit the Report of Disbursement, at the lower portion of the Report of Disbursement (RD) is a summary of the cash transactions for the month. It contains the beginning balance, total cash received by the agency, total disbursements and the ending cash in bank balance.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement to properly monitor the Cash in Bank Balance.
We recommended to call the attention of all concerned employees with unsettled Suspensions/Disallowances to settle the same within the reglementary period of time.

We recommended that the management should submit supporting documents in the implementation of programs, projects and activities (PPAs) expenses. It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to verify the completeness of the attached supporting documents before payment is made in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations in compliance to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

The Auditor reminded the management to submit Contract documents for infrastructure projects within five (5) working days from the date the contract was perfected in compliance with the provision of Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001, resulting to the delay of the Auditor in conducting review and evaluation and informing the management of some defects/deficiencies.

The Municipal Accountant should also submit the Report of Disbursement, at the lower portion of the Report of Disbursement (RD) is a summary of the ending cash transactions for the month. It contains the beginning balance, total cash received by the agency, total disbursements and the ending cash in bank balance.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement to properly monitor the Cash in Bank Balance.
observations.reason: Bank Reconciliatio n Statement were not prepared due to bank’s delay in issuing bank statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Property, Plant and Equipment totaling to P 59,190,165.92 ON ALL FUNDS must be evaluated, thus, submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in violation to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 105 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines is deemed necessary.
observations.reason: The management have not yet created an inventory team to commence inventory of PPE.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) To give justification and reliability to the amount of the account
- Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) totaling ₱ 66,442,110.15 on
all funds;
b) Inventory Committee shall prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, in compliance with
Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume
I;
c) General Services Officer shall maintain and update his property
cards per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant
to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
d) Municipal Accountant shall maintain and update his property
ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
e) Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant shall reconcile their property cards and property
ledger cards, respectively; and
f) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed
physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the
RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each
year.

2.7 Therefore, we highly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation
that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank
Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance
with Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No.
96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

3.3 We recommended to call the attention of all concerned employees
with unsettled Notice of Suspensions/Disallowances to settle the same
within the reglementary period of time.

4.6 We recommended that the management should submit additional
supporting documents in the implementation of programs, projects
and activities (PPAs) and expenses. It is also recommended that the
Municipal Accountant be required to verify the completeness of the
attached supporting documents before payment is made in
compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations in compliance
to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

5.10 We recommend the Municipality to:
a. Instruct the GSO to keep and maintain Stock Cards for each and
every supply to have an adequate monitoring and immediate
information of supplies on hand;
b. Require the Accounting Division to keep and maintain Supplies
Ledger Cards to record supplies received, issued and the balance
both in quantity and amount;
c. Ensure proper coordination between GSO and Accounting
Division to have an updated and reconciled balances in their Stock
Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively; and
d. Prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) for Calendar Year 2021, in compliance with Section
121 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for
Local Government Units, Volume I.
We recommend the management to utilize the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Calamity Fund) in accordance of COA Circular 2012-002 dated Sept. 12, 2012 and RA 10121,12.24.

• Notify the public through signboards of their projects at its location and to provide the Commission on Audit the list of the on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year; and
• Adhere to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004.

The management should fully and properly document all expenses incurred for payment of catering services, meals and snacks for meetings, conferences and other occasions in consonance to Section 7.1 of R.A. 9184 and Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.

The Auditor reminded the management to submit Contract documents for infrastructure projects within five (5) working days from the date the contract was perfected in compliance with the provision of Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001, resulting to the delay of the Auditor in conducting review and evaluation and informing the management of some defects/deficiencies.
observations.reason: Some where not yet submitted due to time constraint.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the management should submit the required documents needed to support the Disbursement Vouchers in paid and for future claims. The responsible employees should see to it that all payments made shall be supported with proper documentation required by the Commission for post audit, pursuant to Section 4 (6) of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommend that the Management shall require the Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment annually taking into consideration the required standards and procedures. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2019 to this Commission, in accordance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 105 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall cause and personally supervise the prompt submission of final reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame prescribed under the existing policies; improve recordkeeping and documentation and effective implementation of internal control and audit system, pursuant to Section 100 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, and other relevant existing regulations.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of reports/statements and other required documents

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five (5) days from issuance as required under Sections 2.1, 3.1, 3.1.3, 3.2, 3.2.1 and 3 of the Commission on Audit Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and the COVID19 pandemic makes it difficult for the LGU to submit

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
The Municipal Accountant should also submit the Report of Disbursement, at the
lower portion of the Report of Disbursement (RD) is a summary of the cash transactions
for the month. It contains the beginning balance, total cash received by the agency, total
disbursements and the ending cash in bank balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to prepare the
Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement to properly monitor the Cash in Bank
Balance pursuant to Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as
the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines

Recommendation
We recommend the management to utilize the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (Calamity Fund) and submit all the documents
necessary for the conduct of audit and examination in accordance of COA
Circular 2012-002 dated Sept. 12, 2012 and Republic Act (R.A.) 10121, otherwise
known as the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010,
and Paragraph 6.3 of Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated
March 25,2013 of the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council
(NDRRMC), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and the
Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG).

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Property, Plant and Equipment totaling to P
59,190,165.92 ON ALL FUNDS must be evaluated, thus, submission of the Report on
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in violation to
Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October
20, 1992, Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 105 of the
Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of
the Philippines is deemed necessary.

Recommendation:
 Notify the public through signboards of their projects at its location and to provide the
Commission on Audit the list of the on-going projects/programs/activities and
those that are to be implemented during the year; and
 Adhere to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004.

Recommendation
The management should fully and properly document all expenses incurred for
payment of catering services, meals and snacks for meetings, conferences in
consonance to Section 7.1 of R.A. 9184 and Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.

Recommendation
The Auditor reminded the management to submit Contract documents for
infrastructure projects within five (5) working days from the date the contract was
perfected in compliance to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001, in order for
the Audit Team to consider necessary review and evaluation of such and informing
the management of some defects/deficiencies, if any.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate refund of the excess claim of traveling expenses and strictly comply with Section 5 of the Executive Order No. 77 dated March 15, 2019 amending Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004.

We recommend that Management shall require the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly the provisions of Sections 4(e) and 5 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 31 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II. Likewise, direct him to prepare and submit immediately the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) as well as the Check Disbursement Journal (CkDJ), Cash Disbursement Journal (CDJ) and Cash Receipts Journal (CRJ).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should limit the procurement of goods and services on a reimbursement basis, unless the circumstances warrant its application and follow strictly the rules and procedures of procurement provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Furthermore, we require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments are made by check directly to the creditors to whom payments are due and withhold the corresponding tax. Regular and recurring expenses should not be paid through reimbursement.

1.4 It is recommended that the management should submit the required documents
needed to support the Disbursement Vouchers paid and for future claims. The responsible
employees should see to it that all payments made shall be supported with proper
documentation required by the Commission for post audit, pursuant to Section 4 (6) of the
Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of
the Philippines.

2.7 We recommend that the Management shall require the Inventory Committee to
conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and Equipment annually taking
into consideration the required standards and procedures. Further, we recommend the
management to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2019 to this Commission, in accordance with Section 156 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Sections 120 and
124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local
Government Units, Volume I and Section 105 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

3.8 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall cause and personally supervise the
prompt submission of final reports/statements and other required reports within the
time frame prescribed under the existing policies; improve recordkeeping and
documentation and effective implementation of internal control system, pursuant to
Section 100 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government
Auditing Code of the Philippines, Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and
Sections 44 and 48 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 and other relevant existing regulations.

4.5 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate refund of the excess
claim of traveling expenses and strictly comply with Section 5 of the Executive Order No. 77
dated March 15, 2019 amending Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004.

5.4 We recommend that Management shall require the Municipal Accountant to adhere
strictly the provisions of Sections 4(e) and 5 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 31 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II. Likewise, direct him to prepare and submit immediately the
Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) as well as the Check Disbursement Journal (CkDJ), Cash
Disbursement Journal (CDJ) and Cash Receipts Journal (CRJ).

6.5 We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and
purchase orders within five (5) days from issuance as required under Sections 2.1, 3.1, 3.1.3,
3.2, 3.2.1 and 3 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February
12, 2009, together with the bidding documents, BAC
resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the
contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of
auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit the Report of Disbursement, at the lower portion of the Report of Disbursement (RD) is a summary of the cash transactions for the month. It contains the beginning balance, total cash received by the agency, total disbursements and the ending cash in bank balance.

Furthermore, the Bookkeeper/Accounting Clerk be instructed to
prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement to properly monitor the
Cash in Bank Balance.

We recommend that the LGU should adopt the perpetual inventory
method in recording procurement of supplies. Moreover, the Accounting and
Supply Offices should revisit the NGAS Manual which details the procedures in
monitoring, controlling, and recording of acquisition and disposal of
inventory.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer shall spearhead in the setting-up of these books of accounts which
are vital in maintenance of the accounts to ensure the proper recording of
transactions and reliability of the reports.

Furthermore, we requested that these financial records shall be made
accessible at reasonable hours to the Auditor or his authorized
representatives when deemed necessary.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should limit the
procurement of goods and services on a reimbursement basis, unless the
circumstances warrant its application and follow strictly the rules and
procedures of procurement provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Furthermore, we require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer to ensure that payments are made by check directly to the creditors
to whom payments are due and withhold the corresponding tax. Regular and
recurring expenses should not be paid through reimbursement.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to
submit immediately all paid disbursement vouchers with original supporting
documents to the Auditor’s Office. Furthermore, the Municipal Mayor shall
cause and personally supervise the prompt submission of final
reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame
prescribed under the existing policies.

We recommend that the Management should require the Inventory
Committee to conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and
Equipment annually taking into consideration the required standards and
procedures. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) CY2018 to
this Commission.

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts
and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA
Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution,
minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to
the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary
in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall spearhead in the setting-up of these books of accounts which are vital in maintenance of the accounts to ensure the proper recording of transactions and reliability of the reports.

The Management shall make sure that the business entities which the LGU is transacting with are duly registered with the BIR to avoid difficulties in withholding taxes; withhold all creditable taxes upon payment of goods and services to the suppliers in order to assure additional income for the government; and remit the same taxes withheld to the BIR on or before the prescribed deadline of filing of remittances

a. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accountant and Treasurer to locate and submit the missing vouchers. The Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals should be submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers/payrolls/of ficial receipts in accordance with the provisions of Section 100 of P.D. 1445.
b. The Treasurer should also be instructed to regularly and promptly submit the Report of checks Issued (RCI) to the Office of the Auditor for proper monitoring of all check issuances.
c. A logbook for vouchers and checks received and released should be maintained by both offices.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to locate the missing DVs but still the Treasurer was unable to regularly submit RCI and other pertinent reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the personnel concerned to settle the Notice of Suspension of P950,607.00, pursuant to  Section 9.4 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Submitted all pertinent documents required for settlement

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all pertinent DVs and other supporting documents related to the above stated transactions from DOH-ARMM Fund shall be submitted for audit examination. Furthermore, that the Management is advised to submit timely all necessary documents for Trust Funds in cases when various transactions have transpired during the year.

We recommend that the Management shall settle the above accounts immediately as possible to avoid the increasing burden on the interest due upon the principal amounts. The Management should abide with the provisions under the GSIS Act and other mandatory provisions to avoid the recurrence of such offense.

We recommend that the Management should abide with the provisions of the new Standard for a reliable presentation of the financial statements. The Management should go over the guidelines provided therein for proper guidance.

We have recommended and management committed to conduct physical inventory of all PPE; and for Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to maintain a complete and updated Subsidiary Ledgers and Property cards, respectively, and have their records reconcile with each other, in accordance with the accounting requirements on property prescribed the NGAS for LGU, Volume I.
